canonical.title: Best YouTube tutorials for the killer-serve shortlist

parent.title: Killer serves for the amateur: a YouTube-anchored roadmap

siblings.title: Killer serves shortlist for amateur play
siblings.summary: Three high-leverage serves a club-level amateur should learn to win cheap points and set up the third-ball attack: short pendulum (backspin/no-spin pair), long fast no-spin to the elbow, and the short forehand tomahawk.

siblings.title: Best YouTube tutorials per serve
siblings.summary: One canonical YouTube video to learn each of the three killer serves (short pendulum + no-spin twin, long fast no-spin, forehand tomahawk), one for the reverse-pendulum upgrade, plus the four channels worth subscribing to.

siblings.title: Solo and partner practice progressions
siblings.summary: How a club-level amateur should build a killer serve: 15 min/day solo with paper targets and 10-in-a-row pressure, then partner drills that walk from fixed feeder return to fully random.

siblings.title: Deception, disguise, and second-contact tricks
siblings.summary: Deep tour of the four deception levers in a table tennis serve — same-motion spin pairs, contact-point disguise, post-contact fakes, and length/direction reversal — plus what receivers actually read so you know what to hide.

sources.title: Short Pendulum Serve Tutorial | Backspin (MLFM)
sources.claim: MLFM's short-pendulum tutorial walks grip, stance, stroke and how to generate backspin specifically for the short pendulum.

sources.title: PingSkills — first step to a great pendulum serve
sources.claim: Drop three lower fingers off the handle, grip with thumb and index only, slide index further onto backhand rubber.

sources.title: Lodziak — serve variation Q&A
sources.claim: Open bat to brush under for backspin; same motion but straighten bat at last moment for no-spin.

sources.title: Sidespin (pendulum) serve — tips and tactics (Lodziak)
sources.claim: Lodziak's highest-viewed pendulum video covering both backspin and topspin variants.

sources.title: 5 Steps To Master The Reverse Pendulum Serve (TTD Academy)
sources.claim: Highest-production-value step-by-step guide for the reverse pendulum, 798K views.

sources.title: Hodges — step-by-step pendulum sequence
sources.claim: Off-table-then-on-table progression: contact near very bottom of ball, first bounce near net.

sources.title: EmRatThich — forehand pendulum tutorial
sources.claim: Loose grip, bat tilted slightly inward, brush lower side, start slow-and-spinny, ~16 cm legal toss.

sources.title: Lodziak — surprising power of a no-spin serve
sources.claim: Contact near the handle where the bat moves slower; same action as heavy-spin without whipping.

sources.title: PingSkills — Backspin and No Spin Serve Variations
sources.claim: Dedicated pair-tutorial built around 3rd-ball-attack opportunities from one motion.

sources.title: PingSkills forum — basic no-spin serve tips
sources.claim: Contact near handle imparts less spin because that part of the blade has a smaller arc.

sources.title: PingSunday — what is a good serve
sources.claim: Change the contact point but keep the same serve motion.

sources.title: TTD forum — float serving
sources.claim: Tap flat (don't brush) on the no-spin twin; stomp the foot to mask the softer ball-blade sound.

sources.title: FOOL Your Opponents: Change the Spin of Your Pendulum Serves (TTD)
sources.claim: 4-minute edit on switching pendulum spin between backspin/sidespin/topspin/no-spin from one motion.

sources.title: FAST Pendulum Serve Tutorial | No Spin (MLFM)
sources.claim: Mimic spin-serve motion at higher pace as the deception layer for the long no-spin variant.

sources.title: How To Serve Fast And Long (PingSkills)
sources.claim: Most-watched short tutorial on the long fast serve, ~1.6M views.

sources.title: How to do a SUPER FAST long serve (Lodziak × Drinkhall)
sources.claim: Pro Paul Drinkhall demonstrates the fast long serve as a 3rd-ball-attack setup, 211K views.

sources.title: 4 Fast & Furious Long Serves (Craig Bryant)
sources.claim: Deep dive covering four variations of long fast serves, 88K views, 14:48.

sources.title: 4 killer serves to destroy your opponents (Lodziak × Bryant)
sources.claim: Most-viewed serve tutorial on YouTube, 9.2M views, 11:37.

sources.title: Lodziak — destroy opponents with long serves
sources.claim: Effective long-serve checklist: fast, low, bounce close to opponent's baseline, placed in a corner or to elbow, disguised, with first bounce on your side very close to your end of the table.

sources.title: Lodziak — how often to use long serves
sources.claim: Up to 80% long serves vs passive players who don't punish them.

sources.title: ExpertTableTennis — how to serve faster
sources.claim: Let the ball drop further than feels comfortable; aim first bounce at your own endline corner.

sources.title: Lodziak — 10 training drill ideas
sources.claim: 3rd Ball Drill 2: serve half-long backspin to feeder's elbow; Receive Drill 2: long sidespin to backhand or elbow.

sources.title: The Table Tennis Service Guy (Craig Bryant) — channel
sources.claim: Channel exclusively about serves, 79.6K subs.

sources.title: Lodziak — Drinkhall fast long serve page
sources.claim: This serve is used to catch opponents out, supporting 2-3-per-game ambush usage.

sources.title: 4 Steps to learn the World's Best Tomahawk Serve (Ovtcharov)
sources.claim: World-class pro who relies on the tomahawk teaches it on his own channel, Jan 2022.

sources.title: Low Tomahawk Serve (PingSkills, Alois Rosario)
sources.claim: Get the body down and contact from below to keep the ball low — essential for a short tomahawk.

sources.title: PingSkills — Tomahawk Serve preview
sources.claim: Action comes naturally to a lot of people; small bat-angle changes disguise spin variations.

sources.title: Tomahawk Serve Tutorial UTT (Adam Bobrow)
sources.claim: Entertainment-flavoured tutorial walking through the throwing-axe motion, Aug 2019.

sources.title: Forehand Tomahawk Serve | Topspin (TableTennisDaily)
sources.claim: Frames serve as 'the axe serve'; brush up the back of the ball after getting low.

sources.title: The EASIEST Serve to Learn — Tomahawk Tutorial (TableTennisDaily)
sources.claim: Most recent mainstream beginner-friendly tutorial; pitches tomahawk as lowest-effort serve to add.

sources.title: Tomahawk Serve by William Henzell (ttEDGE)
sources.claim: 3-time Olympian's slow, low, hard-to-attack short tomahawk — pro reference, March 2015.

sources.title: The Forehand Tomahawk Serve (Samson Dubina)
sources.claim: Right-to-left tip-up motion; serve from forehand side into receiver's wide forehand for max break angle.

sources.title: ExpertTableTennis — Tomahawk Serve
sources.claim: Lift elbow, extend arm, throw the tomahawk; contact upper-right rear, finish underneath; short to forehand 95% of the time.

sources.title: Larry Hodges — tomahawk strategic value
sources.claim: Tremendous sidespin breaks away from the receiver, frequently causing a last-second lunge.

sources.title: TTD Academy — 5 steps to master reverse pendulum (article)
sources.claim: Wrist-only feel, add forearm with elbow lifted, layer waist/shoulder, vary contact point, drill solo.

sources.title: Learning KILLER Reverse Pendulum Serve (MLFM)
sources.claim: Popular alternative walkthrough emphasising deadly-spin angle, 8:30, ~315K views.

sources.title: Learn the Backspin Reverse Pendulum Serve (PingSkills)
sources.claim: Focused short on the hardest variation — genuine backspin from reverse motion, ~2.57M views.

sources.title: Pendulum & Reverse Pendulum Serve (PingSkills)
sources.claim: Side-by-side visual contrast — useful for upgrading, ~797K views, Apr 2008.

sources.title: Master the Reverse Pendulum Serve (Lodziak × Tyla Anderson)
sources.claim: Technique, grip, spin variation, length, placement, plus return-challenge segment.

sources.title: Zhang Jike's Reverse Pendulum Serve | Tutorial & Secret (Ti Long Club)
sources.claim: Pro-player breakdown of the most-imitated reverse pendulum action, ~160K views, 6:57.

sources.title: Why Ma Long has very good pendulum serve (PingSunday)
sources.claim: Ma Long's short pendulum sets up his 3rd-ball attack, ~76K views, 5:36.

sources.title: PingSunday — reverse pendulum serve
sources.claim: Racket moves outside-inward; spin direction varies with racket angle and contact point. Heavily used by Zhang Jike.

sources.title: Zhang Jike Reverse Pendulum Serve Slow Motion
sources.claim: Pure slow-motion reference of the canonical pro action, ~65K views.

sources.title: PingSkills — about
sources.claim: Run by Jeff Plumb (Sydney 2000 Olympian) and Alois Rosario (Australian Olympic/Paralympic coach 2012–2024).

sources.title: PingSkills — Instagram (subs verification)
sources.claim: PingSkills YouTube has approximately 200K subscribers.

sources.title: Tom Lodziak — about
sources.claim: TT England Level 2 coach, trained under Richard Prause; 275K subs, 50M views.

sources.title: Lodziak — epic sidespin serve tutorial
sources.claim: Lodziak's serve content explicitly targets amateur and league players.

sources.title: Dan Ives — LinkedIn
sources.claim: TableTennisDaily founder; 15+ years coaching experience; first-class Sports Coaching degree.

sources.title: TTD forum — Andrew Baggaley interview
sources.claim: Multiple English national champion; world No. 139, England No. 1.

sources.title: PingSunday — about EmRatThich
sources.claim: Vietnamese-born, France-based coach with Sorbonne PhD; 250K subs.

sources.title: Adam Bobrow — Wikipedia
sources.claim: Official ITTF commentator since 2014; ~1.43M subscribers.

sources.title: Pongfinity — Wikipedia
sources.claim: Finnish trick-shot trio; 4.67M subscribers — largest table-tennis channel.

sources.title: Brian Pace — Butterfly
sources.claim: USATT Certified National Coach with peak rating 2610.

sources.title: Racket Insight — top YouTube channels
sources.claim: Independent rankings consistently put PingSkills, Lodziak, TableTennisDaily and PingSunday at top tier.
